captures.caption: Unit 2's ship pushed right: the 8-bit X position can't pass 255, so about seven-eighths across it wraps straight back to the left edge — the right strip of screen is unreachable.
captures.alt: The ship slides right, then suddenly jumps back to the left edge when its X position wraps past 255, never reaching the rightmost part of the screen.

captures.caption: With the 9th bit, pushing right carries the ship smoothly past the old halfway point all the way to the right edge — the full screen width is reachable now.
captures.alt: The ship slides right and continues smoothly past where it used to wrap, reaching the far right edge of the screen without jumping.

captures.caption: Clamped: the ship runs to the right edge and stops dead, then to the left edge and stops — no wrapping, always on screen.
captures.alt: The ship moves to the right edge and halts there, then moves to the left edge and halts, staying fully visible at both extremes instead of wrapping.
